metaTitle: Rat Extermination NYC | Rat Control & Removal | The Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional rat extermination in NYC. Trapping, baiting, and exclusion. Free inspection. Same-day available. Licensed NYS DEC exterminators. Call now.
h1: Rat Extermination in NYC — Eliminated and Kept Out
heroParagraph: NYC's rat population is legendary — and for good reason. Norway rats have colonized the city's subway system, trash infrastructure, and building foundations for over 200 years. Getting rid of the rats you have is only half the battle. Keeping new ones out is the other. Our rat control programs combine strategic trapping, tamper-resistant bait stations, and structural exclusion work to eliminate current infestations and seal every entry point against future ones.
bodyParagraphs: Norway rats are the dominant rat species in New York City. They're large — up to 16 inches including the tail — brown or gray, and almost exclusively ground-level. They burrow near foundations, dumpsters, subway infrastructure, and anywhere with reliable food sources. House mice are a separate species: much smaller, gray, and found on every floor of every building type in the city. Both gnaw through electrical wiring, contaminate food, and carry disease. Both reproduce fast enough to turn a small problem into a serious infestation within weeks.
signs: Droppings — dark, banana-shaped, 3/4 inch long — along walls and near food sources
Gnaw marks on food packaging, wood, and electrical wiring insulation
Burrows near the foundation, under concrete, or along fence lines
Grease rub marks — dark smears on walls where rats repeatedly travel
Scratching, gnawing, or squeaking sounds in walls at night
Live or dead rats — if you see them, the population is large
treatmentApproach: Rat control starts with a property inspection to identify burrow locations, entry points, travel paths, and food sources. Treatment includes snap traps and tamper-resistant bait stations placed strategically along travel routes, followed by exclusion work to seal every identified entry point with steel mesh, concrete, and expanding foam. We document every access point sealed and schedule follow-up visits to clear traps, monitor activity, and confirm elimination. For commercial properties and restaurant clients, we provide full DOH-compliant service documentation.

faqs.q: How do rats get into NYC buildings?
faqs.a: Rats enter through gaps as small as 1/2 inch — foundation cracks, gaps around pipes and conduits, damaged basement windows, open utility access points, and deteriorated door sweeps. In buildings with shared basement spaces, rats travel freely between units. Exclusion — physically sealing these entry points — is the only permanent solution.

faqs.q: Can I get rid of rats myself?
faqs.a: Retail snap traps can catch individual rats, but they don't address the colony or the entry points. Without exclusion work, new rats replace eliminated ones within days. Professional treatment addresses the full infestation and seals entry points to prevent reinfestation.

faqs.q: How much does rat extermination cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Rat control typically runs $200–$600 for initial treatment and exclusion of a residential property. Larger properties, commercial spaces, or significant exclusion work are quoted after inspection. Rodent-proofing (comprehensive structural exclusion) is quoted separately at $500–$3,000 depending on scope.

faqs.q: How long does rat extermination take?
faqs.a: Active rat control typically requires 2–4 visits over 3–4 weeks to eliminate the current population and confirm all entry points are sealed. Signs of activity decrease significantly within the first week of treatment.
